title: cerastium fontanum f. glutinosum
intro: Cerastium fontanum f. glutinosum, also known as sticky mouse-ear or sticky cranesbill, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of this plant include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to soothe skin irritations and wounds. In herbal preparations, Cerastium fontanum f. glutinosum can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat colds, sore throats, and other respiratory problems.
